diagrams.title: Flynn's Taxonomy Classification Matrix
diagrams.description: A 2×2 matrix categorizing computer architectures based on instruction and data streams
elements: SISD (Single Instruction Single Data) - Row 1 Col 1
SIMD (Single Instruction Multiple Data) - Row 1 Col 2
MISD (Multiple Instruction Single Data) - Row 2 Col 1
MIMD (Multiple Instruction Multiple Data) - Row 2 Col 2
diagrams.purpose: Visualize all four categories of Flynn's Taxonomy in a single matrix to understand classification criteria and relationships between different parallel architectures

diagrams.title: Shared Memory vs Distributed Memory Architecture
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison of memory organizations in parallel systems
elements: Shared Memory: Processors → Shared Bus → Unified Memory
Distributed Memory: Processor-Memory pairs connected via Interconnection Network
Communication: Implicit (read/write shared variables) vs Explicit (message passing)
diagrams.purpose: Compare the two fundamental memory organization models showing physical layout, communication mechanisms, and trade-offs in complexity versus scalability

diagrams.title: Amdahl's Law Speedup Curve
diagrams.description: Graph showing theoretical speedup versus number of processors for different parallel fractions
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how parallel fraction limits maximum achievable speedup regardless of processor count - demonstrates the critical importance of minimizing serial portions of code
